prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "反向北欧弯举" (inverse Nordic curl) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-descent in an inverse Nordic curl: kneeling on the ground with both knees on a soft pad, shins flat on the ground, toes pointed, and ankles flat; the body is leaning backward as a single straight rigid line from knees to head, currently at about 45 degrees from vertical (descending under control); arms crossed over the chest or extended forward for counterbalance; the quadriceps work eccentrically to control the descent; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps (under heavy eccentric load). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors and core muscles for spinal stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps under eccentric load; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
